---
title: "Exploring Classic R Datasets"
author:
  - name: Russ Blessing
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    corresponding: true
    email: rblessing@unc.edu
    affiliations:
      - UNC Chapel Hill
date: today
abstract: |
  A short reproducible analysis of the iris and mtcars datasets,
  demonstrating how figures generated in a separate analysis notebook
  can be embedded directly into a Quarto manuscript. All figures
  are produced with ggplot2 and sourced from notebooks/analysis.qmd.
keywords: [reproducible research, ggplot2, iris, mtcars]
license: CC BY 4.0
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
  pdf: default
  docx: default
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

This manuscript demonstrates a reproducible workflow using Quarto.
All figures are generated in a dedicated analysis notebook
(`notebooks/analysis.qmd`) and embedded here using the
`{{< embed >}}` shortcode. Updating the notebook automatically
updates this manuscript — no copy-paste required.

## Iris Dataset

The `iris` dataset contains measurements of sepal and petal
dimensions for 150 flowers across three species: *setosa*,
*versicolor*, and *virginica* [@anderson1935].

### Sepal Dimensions

@fig-iris-sepal shows a clear separation between species in
sepal space, with *setosa* occupying a distinct region of
high width relative to length.

{{< embed notebooks/analysis.qmd#fig-iris-sepal >}}

### Petal Length

Species separation is even more pronounced in petal length
(@fig-iris-petal), making it a reliable discriminating variable.

{{< embed notebooks/analysis.qmd#fig-iris-petal >}}

## Motor Trend Cars

The `mtcars` dataset records fuel efficiency and physical
characteristics for 32 automobiles from the 1974 Motor Trend
magazine [@henderson1981]. @fig-mtcars illustrates the well-known
negative relationship between vehicle weight and fuel efficiency,
stratified by cylinder count.

{{< embed notebooks/analysis.qmd#fig-mtcars >}}

## Conclusion

This document was rendered entirely from plain-text source files.
The figures above were produced in `notebooks/analysis.qmd` and
embedded without manual copying. Any change to the analysis
notebook is reflected here on the next render.

## References
